item.title: 人类与五角大楼的争议引发战场人工智能边界之争
item.description: 人工智能控制的武器，自主性达到什么程度才算过头？【原文：How much autonomy is too much for weapons directed by AI?】

item.title: 中国寄望于消费者推动下一阶段的经济增长
item.description: 彭博经济研究预计，到2030年消费将上升至占国内生产总值的46%。【原文：Bloomberg Economics sees consumption rising to make up 46% of GDP by 2030】

item.title: 特朗普猛烈抨击西班牙，威胁实施贸易禁运
item.description: 西班牙官员予以斥责，称他必须遵守国际贸易规则。【原文：Spanish officials issued a rebuke, saying he must follow the rules of international trade】

item.title: 电价飙升迫使美国政界人士寻求解决方案
item.description: 美国政界人士正争相采取措施控制电价飙升，这场能源危机可能颠覆今年的国会选举格局。【原文：US politicians are scrambling to tame the soaring cost of electricity that threatens to upend this year’s congressional elections.】

item.title: 房主持房12年不愿出售，扼杀美国住房市场
item.description: 美国房主们正选择按兵不动，这一趋势正助推房价持续攀升，令许多人望而却步。【原文：American homeowners are staying put, a trend that’s helping drive home prices out of reach for many.】

item.title: 荷兰基金因税制改革面临41亿欧元赎回申请
item.description: 荷兰房地产基金Vesteda Residential表示，在法律变更导致外国投资者面临更高的房地产投资税负后，其几乎所有投资者都计划减持或出售所持股份。【原文：Dutch real estate fund Vesteda Residential said almost all its investors are planning to reduce or sell their holding, after a change in the law left foreign investors facing higher taxes on real estate investments.】

item.title: 图表观察者担忧战争忧虑加剧标普500指数修正风险
item.description: 连续两日，美国股市开盘即大幅下挫，中东战事令投资者风险偏好骤降。午盘时分，逢低买盘两次出手救市，大幅收复早盘失地。【原文：For two straight days, US stocks have started deep in the red as the war in the Middle East turned investors risk averse. Both times dip buyers rode to the rescue midday, wiping out much of the earlier drops.】

item.title: 博通财报成为最新试图攀越人工智能恐惧高墙的企业
item.description: 华尔街预计博通公司将在周三盘后公布强劲财报，但根据近期交易走势，即便业绩超出预期也可能不足以扭转该股持续数月的低迷态势。【原文：Wall Street is expecting Broadcom Inc. to report strong earnings after the close on Wednesday, but based on recent trading patterns, even surpassing that high bar may not be enough to reverse the stock’s months-long slump.】

item.title: 挪威房价意外下跌，买家转趋谨慎
item.description: 挪威核心房价意外出现十个月来首次下跌，表明购房者正将央行降息概率降低的预期纳入考量。【原文：Norway’s underlying home prices surprised with a first decline in 10 months, suggesting buyers are factoring in a lower likelihood of central bank interest-rate cuts.】

item.title: 德国任命费尔贝迈尔为默茨经济事务新顾问
item.description: 德国任命加布里埃尔·费尔贝迈尔接替乌尔里克·马尔门迪尔，担任政府经济专家委员会成员。该委员会负责为政府提供经济政策建议。【原文：Germany picked Gabriel Felbermayr to succeed Ulrike Malmendier on the council of economic experts, which advises the government.】

item.title: 巴克莱银行被倒闭的英国贷款机构MFS拖欠约5亿英镑
item.description: 知情人士透露，巴克莱银行约有5亿英镑（合6.69亿美元）债权未收回，这些债务由与Market Financial Solutions Ltd.相关的公司所欠。该公司是英国倒闭抵押贷款机构最大的风险敞口之一。【原文：Barclays Plc is owed about £500 million ($669 million) by companies associated with Market Financial Solutions Ltd., one of the biggest exposures to the collapsed UK mortgage lender, a person familiar with the matter said.】

item.title: 高盛策略师建议逢低买入：伊朗与人工智能双重利好
item.description: 高盛集团策略师表示，投资者应将股市的任何回调视为买入良机，而非熊市来临的信号。【原文：Investors should see any correction in equities as a buying opportunity, rather than signaling the start of a bear market, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategists.】

item.title: 今年获利股票交易在战时抛售中首当其冲
item.description:
亚洲股市暴跌，部分原因是该地区经济依赖进口石油。【原文：

Asian stocks are plunging, in part because the region’s economies depend on imported oil.

】

item.title: Whoop计划扩充75%员工规模，为即将到来的IPO加速发展
item.description: Whoop公司计划今年将员工规模扩大75%，以推动业务增长，为潜在的首次公开募股（IPO）做准备。该公司以生产无屏幕健身手环而闻名。【原文：Whoop Inc., the maker of popular screenless fitness bands, plans to increase its staff by as much as 75% this year to fuel growth ahead of a potential initial public offering.】

item.title: 欧元区失业率意外降至历史最低水平
item.description: 欧元区失业率意外降至历史最低水平，因经济扩张速度超出预期。【原文：Unemployment in the euro area unexpectedly dropped to a record low as economic expansion outpaces forecasts.】

item.title: 美元跌至一个月低点，因伊朗接触计划报告
item.description: 在《纽约时报》报道称伊朗特工曾提出讨论结束战争的条件后，美元汇率创下近一个月来最大跌幅。【原文：The dollar fell the most in nearly a month after after a New York Times report said Iranian operatives had offered to discuss terms for ending the war.】

item.title: 印度尿素生产商削减产量，因伊朗战争扰乱液化天然气供应
item.description: 由于中东地区战事导致卡塔尔液化天然气供应中断，印度化肥制造商正开始削减产量。液化天然气是化肥生产的关键原料。【原文：Fertilizer manufacturers in India are beginning to cut output after Qatari supplies of liquefied natural gas, a key feedstock, were suspended due to hostilities in the Middle East.】

item.title: 若乌克兰教训得以吸取，能源股反弹才刚开始
item.description: 上次武装冲突颠覆全球能源经济时，原油价格曾飙升至每桶100美元以上，石油和天然气生产商的股价也持续数月上涨。随着中东战火蔓延，类似走势或许正在重演。【原文：The last time an armed conflict upended the global energy economy, crude spiked past $100 and shares in oil and gas producers rallied for months. A similar trajectory might be unfolding as war rages in the Middle East.】

item.title: 印度炼油商MRPL因伊朗战争影响原料供应暂停燃料出口
item.description: 印度芒格洛尔炼油和石化公司已通知客户，将暂停石油产品出口，因中东地区冲突扩大导致波斯湾原油运输中断。【原文：India’s Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals Ltd. has told customers it will suspend oil product exports, as a widening conflict in the Middle East disrupts crude shipments from the Persian Gulf.】

item.title: 摩根士丹利预计国防领域将迎来巨额资金流入，因基金下调投资限制
item.description: 随着地缘政治紧张局势和全面战争重新定义ESG投资者的内涵，以可持续投资为目标的基金正逐渐成为国防产业的重要支持力量。【原文：Funds with sustainable investing goals are set to become meaningful backers of the defense industry, as geopolitical tensions and outright war redefine what it means to be an ESG investor.】

item.title: 加纳5G平台正式启用，力争一年内实现70%覆盖率
item.description: 加纳已在部分城市开通首个5G网络，为移动运营商开始提供超高速无线服务铺平道路。【原文：Ghana has brought its first 5G network online in selected cities, paving the way for mobile operators to begin offering ultra-fast wireless services.】

item.title: F-35以色列战机在空战中首次击落敌机
item.description: 以色列国防军称，一架以色列F-35I战斗机在德黑兰上空击落了一架伊朗雅克-130多用途喷气机，这标志着美国制造的隐形战机首次在空战中击落有人驾驶飞机。【原文：An Israeli F-35I fighter shot down an Iranian Yak-130 multirole jet over Tehran, the Israel Defense Forces said, marking the US-made stealth jet’s first known air-to-air victory against a crewed aircraft.】

item.title: 加纳通胀推升降息预期，战争阴云笼罩前景
item.description: 加纳2月份通胀率降至近三十年来的最低水平，这增强了市场对该国连续第五次降息的预期。尽管中东冲突可能重燃全球价格压力并蒙上前景阴霾。【原文：Ghana’s inflation rate fell to a near three-decade low in February, bolstering expectations for a fifth straight rate cut even as conflict in the Middle East threatens to rekindle global price pressures and cloud the outlook.】

item.title: 捷克通胀意外放缓，伊朗战争带来新风险
item.description: 捷克通胀率在2月意外进一步放缓，但伊朗战争引发的能源冲击可能限制央行重启降息的空间。【原文：Czech inflation unexpectedly slowed further in February but the energy shock from the Iran war may limit the central bank’s room for resuming interest rate cuts.】

item.title: 普氏排除海湾装货后中东油价飙升
item.description: 中东迪拜基准油价飙升，因普氏能源资讯旗下主要交易平台出现令人咋舌的竞价——此前该机构决定将波斯湾装运的油品排除在其定价体系之外。【原文：The price of Middle East’s Dubai benchmark soared as bids were placed at eye-watering levels on a key trading platform run by Platts, after a decision to exclude grades loading in the Persian Gulf from its pricing.】

item.title: 巴基斯坦因战争扰乱供应，削减部分工业买家的天然气供应
item.description: 巴基斯坦最大的天然气分销商即将削减对部分工业客户的供气量，这表明这个高度依赖卡塔尔能源出口的经济体正面临日益严峻的压力。【原文：Pakistan’s largest gas distributor is set to cut supplies to some of its industrial customers, suggesting growing strain in one of the economies most dependent on energy exports from Qatar.】

item.title: 瑞典敦促公民备好现金以防战争或危机
item.description: 瑞典央行表示，所有瑞典成年人应在家中备足一周的现金，以便在战争或其他危机发生时购买食品、药品及其他必需品。【原文：All Swedish adults should hold a week’s worth of cash at home so they can buy food, medicine and other essentials in case of a war or other crisis, Sweden’s central bank said.】

item.title: 印度因大豆油溢价扩大而取消更多大豆油货运
item.description: 作为全球最大的食用油进口国，印度已取消更多大豆油货运订单，因该产品成本较替代品急剧攀升。【原文：India, the world’s top edible oil importer, has canceled more soybean oil cargoes as costs for the product have risen sharply compared with alternatives.】

item.title: 英国最高法院裁定：西班牙在太阳能纠纷中不得主张国家豁免权
item.description: 英国最高法院作出裁决后，可再生能源投资者距离夺取西班牙政府在伦敦富裕地段拥有的房产又近了一步，此举旨在执行一项价值1.2亿欧元（约合1.39亿美元）的仲裁裁决。【原文：Renewable energy investors are one step closer to seizing a property owned by the Spanish government in an affluent part of London, following a UK Supreme Court ruling, in order to enforce a €120 million ($139 million) arbitration award.】

item.title: 欧洲央行博客称，人工智能尚未导致欧洲企业裁员
item.description: 欧洲央行博客文章指出，迄今为止，人工智能尚未对欧元区就业产生负面影响，该技术的重度使用者甚至增加了员工数量。【原文：Artificial intelligence is so far having no negative impact on euro-zone employment, with the heaviest users of the technology even adding staff, according to a European Central Bank blog post.】
